summary: Postinstall script harvests SSH keys, AWS IAM credentials, and npm tokens to hardcoded remote IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a credential-harvesting and exfiltration sequence against the installer's machine. It chmods and reads /root/.ssh/id_rsa, attempts SSH using that key as multiple users against the hardcoded external IP 154.57.164.64, runs `ssh-keygen -lf` on the public key, and reads /root/.npmrc and /root/flag. It also curls AWS ECS/IMDS link-local endpoints (http://169.254.170.2/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/ and IPv6 fd00:ec2::254) to harvest IAM role credentials. Captured stdout is base64-encoded into a YAML body and PUT to http://154.57.164.64:32271/api/modules/{ECT-839201,ECT-472839} over plain HTTP. The script also persistently redirects the host's npm registry by running `npm config set registry http://registry.spectral-corsair.htb:32271/`, pivoting all future installs through attacker infrastructure. Execution is gated by anti-analysis checks: skip CI/GitHub Actions, skip common mirrors (npmmirror, tencent, taobao, huawei, ustc, cnpm), require a 12-hex-char container hostname and a cwd containing /app — designed to fire only on the intended target containers and avoid scanners.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
